title: Fix rate limit check — validate_rate_limits returns allowed=True at limit
description: policies/engine.py validate_rate_limits() returns allowed=True when the post count equals the limit (3/3). The test test_blocked_when_at_limit expects allowed=False when current == limit. This is a live bug — the system can exceed rate limits.
requirements: validate_rate_limits must return allowed=False when current count >= limit
Must not break validate_rate_limits for counts below the limit
done_criteria: All other rate limit tests still pass
context: The test inserts 3 posts for bluesky (max_posts_per_day=3) and expects allowed=False. Currently returns True. Likely an off-by-one: using > instead of >= in the comparison.

feedback_history.reason: Code already correct in clean worktree — test fails due to test environment issue, not code bug
